req.title: Export employee directory
req.statement: An hr-admin shall export the employee directory as CSV with PII redacted for employees who opted out of public directory.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers three distinct concerns: (1) a valid hr-admin export returns a well-formed CSV with PII redacted for opted-out employees (positive, EP/DT), (2) a non-admin caller is rejected with 403 (negative, NEG), and (3) a caller who has just hit the rate-limit ceiling receives 429 (boundary, BVA). A fourth test covering the decision-table interaction between opt-in vs opt-out rows in the same CSV payload ensures the redaction logic is exercised for both partitions in one run. Together the four tests satisfy the minimum three-case requirement while achieving full coverage of the stated error pathways and expected outcomes.

tests.name: test_hr_admin_export_returns_csv_with_pii_redacted
preconditions: At least one employee has opted out of public directory
At least one employee has NOT opted out
expected: HTTP 200 with Content-Type text/csv or application/octet-stream
Response body is valid CSV with a header row
Opted-out employee rows have PII fields replaced with REDACTED placeholder
Non-opted-out employee rows contain real PII values
trace: R-WEB-082 -> Expected outcomes: CSV streamed; PII redacted per opt-out
tests.executable:
import pytest
import csv
import io

def test_hr_admin_export_returns_csv_with_pii_redacted(
    hr_admin_token,          # <fixture> returns valid bearer token
    api_client,              # <fixture> requests.Session pointed at hr-app
    opted_out_employee_id,   # <fixture> employee ID with opt-out=True
    opted_in_employee_id,    # <fixture> employee ID with opt-out=False
):
    resp = api_client.get(
        "/api/employees/export.csv",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}"},
        stream=True,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    ct = resp.headers.get("Content-Type", "")
    assert "csv" in ct or "octet-stream" in ct

    reader = csv.DictReader(io.StringIO(resp.text))
    rows = list(reader)
    assert len(rows) > 0, "CSV must contain at least one data row"

    opted_out = [r for r in rows if r.get("id") == str(opted_out_employee_id)]
    assert opted_out, "Opted-out employee must appear in export"
    assert opted_out[0]["email"] == "REDACTED"
    assert opted_out[0]["phone"] == "REDACTED"

    opted_in = [r for r in rows if r.get("id") == str(opted_in_employee_id)]
    assert opted_in, "Opted-in employee must appear in export"
    assert opted_in[0]["email"] != "REDACTED"

tests.name: test_non_admin_export_returns_403
preconditions: Caller is authenticated but has role:employee (not hr-admin)
expected: Response body contains error indicator (no CSV data leaked)
trace: R-WEB-082 -> Error pathways: 403 if not hr-admin
tests.executable:
def test_non_admin_export_returns_403(
    employee_token,   # <fixture> bearer token for role:employee
    api_client,       # <fixture>
):
    resp = api_client.get(
        "/api/employees/export.csv",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {employee_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    ct = resp.headers.get("Content-Type", "")
    assert "csv" not in ct, "CSV must not be returned to unauthorized caller"
    body = resp.text.lower()
    assert any(kw in body for kw in ["forbidden", "unauthorized", "error"])

tests.name: test_export_returns_429_when_rate_limit_exceeded
preconditions: Rate-limit counter for this caller is at limit - 1 (one request below cap)
actions: GET /api/employees/export.csv exactly at the rate-limit ceiling (N+1 request after N allowed)
Inspect response of the request that crosses the boundary
expected: The N+1 request returns HTTP 429 Too Many Requests
trace: BVA applied to the exact transition point at the rate-limit boundary
tests.executable:
def test_export_returns_429_when_rate_limit_exceeded(
    hr_admin_token,          # <fixture>
    api_client,              # <fixture>
    reset_rate_limit,        # <fixture> resets limiter counter before test
    rate_limit_cap,          # <fixture> int: max allowed requests per window
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}"}
    url = "/api/employees/export.csv"

    # exhaust the allowed quota (boundary: last permitted request)
    for i in range(rate_limit_cap):
        r = api_client.get(url, headers=headers)
        assert r.status_code == 200, f"Request {i+1} should succeed before cap"

    # one request beyond the boundary
    over_limit = api_client.get(url, headers=headers)
    assert over_limit.status_code == 429
    assert "Retry-After" in over_limit.headers, "429 must include Retry-After"

tests.name: test_export_decision_table_opt_in_vs_opt_out_redaction
preconditions: Directory contains employees covering all four opt-out/PII combinations: opted-out with email+phone, opted-in with email+phone
actions: Parse CSV and evaluate each row against opt-out flag
expected: Row where opt_out=true: email==REDACTED AND phone==REDACTED
Row where opt_out=false: email!=REDACTED AND phone!=REDACTED
No row is missing from the export regardless of opt-out status
trace: R-WEB-082 -> Expected outcomes: PII redacted per opt-out
DT: all combinations of opt-out flag x PII field presence
tests.executable:
import csv
import io

def test_export_decision_table_opt_in_vs_opt_out_redaction(
    hr_admin_token,            # <fixture>
    api_client,                # <fixture>
    seeded_directory,          # <fixture> returns {"opted_out": [...ids], "opted_in": [...ids]}
):
    resp = api_client.get(
        "/api/employees/export.csv",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200

    rows = list(csv.DictReader(io.StringIO(resp.text)))
    row_index = {r["id"]: r for r in rows}

    for eid in seeded_directory["opted_out"]:
        row = row_index.get(str(eid))
        assert row is not None, f"Opted-out employee {eid} missing from export"
        assert row["email"] == "REDACTED", f"email not redacted for {eid}"
        assert row["phone"] == "REDACTED", f"phone not redacted for {eid}"

    for eid in seeded_directory["opted_in"]:
        row = row_index.get(str(eid))
        assert row is not None, f"Opted-in employee {eid} missing from export"
        assert row["email"] != "REDACTED", f"email wrongly redacted for {eid}"
        assert row["phone"] != "REDACTED", f"phone wrongly redacted for {eid}"
